libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, external service, API key, environment variable, or global system property appears required. Meaningful tests should use a local Spring application context and local test beans/tool callbacks; optionally set the Spring property `spring.ai.tools.observations.include-content=true` inside the test context to exercise the conditional observation-filter bean.

deterministic_setup.reason: The auto-configuration is conditional on Spring AI ChatModel and creates ToolCallingManager, ToolCallbackResolver, ToolCallbackProvider, and related tool-calling classes from spring-ai-model; this dependency is optional in the target POM and will not be pulled by the normal scaffold.

The target artifact is a Spring Boot auto-configuration module using AutoConfiguration, ConditionalOnClass, ConditionalOnMissingBean, ConditionalOnProperty, and configuration-properties support; this dependency is optional in the target POM and is needed to exercise the library as normally used.

risks: If tests use Spring Boot's ApplicationContextRunner convenience API, they may also need a test-only Spring Boot test dependency, but that is a test harness choice rather than a requirement of the library artifact itself.
Avoid invoking real AI model providers; this module only configures common tool-calling infrastructure and can be covered with local stubs.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a Spring Boot auto-configuration module whose key runtime dependencies are declared optional, so the normal scaffold dependency on the target artifact alone is unlikely to provide enough classpath for meaningful Spring AI tool-calling auto-configuration coverage.
